Rule: Route Handler / Server Action → Service → Repository (Prisma)
Detail: Handlers delegate to Services; Services handle logic; Repos handle DB via Prisma ORM

Rule: Max ~30 lines per function — split logic into private helpers
1 exported function = 1 Service method = 1 clear job
Use DTO/serializer to map Prisma model to API response shape
Detail: Never expose raw Prisma model (may contain password hash etc.)

Rule: Route Handlers in app/api/[resource]/route.ts — Server Actions in app/actions/[resource].ts
{ success: boolean, message: string, data: T | null }
{ data: T[], meta: { pagination: { page, limit, total, pages } } }
{ success: false, message: string, errors?: Record<string,string> }
camelCase for all fields; nest relations as objects (not _id)
Detail: user: { id name avatar } not userId

Rule: Use NextResponse.json() with explicit status for all route handlers
Wrap ALL Route Handler and Server Action bodies in try/catch
Use apiError(message, status) helper for consistent error response
Detail: Never throw unhandled errors in production

Rule: Log exceptions: console.error('[Module] action:', error)
Detail: Track errors — integrate with Sentry/Axiom in production

Rule: Return user-friendly message; log technical detail server-side
Detail: Never expose stack trace to client

Rule: Never query inside forEach/map loops — use Prisma include/select with where
Detail: Use findMany with include for relations

Rule: Always select specific fields in Prisma: select: { id: true, title: true, slug: true, thumb: true }
Exclude content/description/longtext from list views — load only in detail/edit
All list endpoints MUST use take/skip — max 20-50 items
Use createMany() / updateMany() for bulk operations
Every foreign key column (userId, storyId) MUST have @@index in Prisma schema
Columns in WHERE or ORDER BY frequently → add @@index
Detail: Composite index for common filter pairs

Rule: Add @@index(['userId']) or @@index(['status'
Avoid holding large arrays in memory in batch jobs
Process large datasets in chunks (500-1000 rows) using cursor-based pagination
Use Next.js Streaming (ReadableStream) for exports — do not load all into RAM
Validate ALL inputs with Zod before processing
Use Prisma ORM parameterized queries — NEVER use $queryRaw with user input
Sanitize user-submitted HTML content before storage/output
Verify JWT/session + role check on every protected route handler
Apply rate limiting on login/register/sensitive endpoints
Next.js Server Actions have built-in CSRF protection — Route Handlers must verify Origin header
Detail: Check request headers on sensitive mutations

Rule: 1. Check server logs → 2. Reproduce with curl/Postman → 3. Add console.log to service → 4. Fix
Enable Prisma query logging in development: log: ['query']
Use Next.js built-in profiler and React DevTools in development (not production)
After fix: test same endpoint + verify response format
Cache frequent reads (settings/categories/rankings) with React cache() or unstable_cache
Load relations in single Prisma query with include/select
Offload heavy tasks (email/image processing) to background job (Inngest or Trigger.dev)
console.error/warn/info('[Module] Action:', detail)
error (exceptions) / warn (edge cases) / info (audit trail)
Integrate Sentry or Axiom for production error tracking
Detail: Never rely on console only in prod
